solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findLUSlength(String a, String b) {
        if (a.equals(b)) {
            return -1;
        }
        return Math.max(a.length(), b.length());
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution checks if the two strings are equal. If they are, then no uncommon subsequence exists, so return -1. Otherwise, the longest uncommon subsequence is the longer of the two strings, since it cannot be a subsequence of the other. This approach is efficient with constant space and linear time.>
